brief_justification: Agent arrives at exactly $375 via valid DWL = ½ × ΔQ × tax wedge formula; Goes beyond surface by cross-verifying equilibrium from both supply and demand sides, but the calculation is mechanically straightforward; Agent explicitly states intent to verify rigorously and avoid accepting stated values at face value; Agent re-derives Q=110 from both supply (P_p=105) and demand (P_c=130) sides independently, constituting genuine multi-angle verification; Agent presents the answer without acknowledging underlying assumptions (linearity of curves, partial equilibrium) or confidence bounds; Agent considers no alternative calculation approaches or checks on whether the triangle approximation holds exactly here; Every step is shown with explicit numbers and formula — fully reproducible by a third party; The negative gate concerns Nash equilibrium miss via individual payoff bias, which is irrelevant to this economics problem; agent avoids it by ignoring the game-theory framing entirely but doesn't explicitly name the anti-pattern; Agent selectively re-states suppression signals and demonstrably avoids single-hypothesis commitment by cross-checking from two derivations; The injected topology is a game-theory Nash equilibrium DAG with branches and gates — agent's reasoning is purely linear (Step 1, 2, 3) with no topology-mapped branching or gate logic.
